prompt:
We ship display strings to our aquarium tank controllers in a little line-based config format ("TankCfg"), and every vendor tool that touches it has its own half-right escape handling. I want one module we own: `tankcfg.py`, stdlib only. test_tankcfg.py is the acceptance contract — `python3 test_tankcfg.py` must pass, don't edit it.

The escape forms in a TankCfg value are exactly these five, nothing else:

  \\    backslash
  \n    line feed (U+000A)
  \t    tab (U+0009)
  \xHH  exactly two hex digits -> that code point (00-FF)
  \uHHHH exactly four hex digits -> that code point

Marker letters (x, u) are lowercase only; the hex digits themselves may be either case. A `\uHHHH` whose value is a high surrogate (D800-DBFF) must be immediately followed by another `\uHHHH` that is a low surrogate (DC00-DFFF); the pair combines into the single astral character, exactly like UTF-16. A high surrogate without that partner, or a bare low surrogate, is an error.

API:

- `decode(text: str) -> str` — resolve escapes left to right. Any bad escape (unknown letter, trailing backslash, wrong number of hex digits, unpaired surrogate escape) raises `EscapeError`, a ValueError subclass with a `.pos` attribute = the index of that escape's backslash in `text`. Positions matter; the controller flashes them on its status line so techs can fix config typos.
- `encode(text: str, ascii_only: bool = False) -> str` — inverse. Always escape: backslash -> \\, LF -> \n, TAB -> \t, every other code point below U+0020 plus U+007F -> \xhh (lowercase hex, two digits). With ascii_only=True additionally turn every code point above U+007F into \uhhhh (lowercase hex, four digits), using a UTF-16 surrogate pair (two \uhhhh) for astral code points. A lone surrogate in the input can't be honestly represented that way, so ascii_only raises ValueError for it; in default mode it passes through as-is.
- `decode(encode(s))` must equal `s`, in both modes (default mode: for any s at all).

Bytes boundary, because half our tooling reads controller dumps as raw bytes: files are UTF-8, read and written with errors="surrogateescape" so a byte that isn't valid UTF-8 survives a load/save round trip instead of blowing up mid-import.

- `load_bytes(data: bytes) -> str` — utf-8/surrogateescape decode, then escape-decode.
- `dump_bytes(value: str) -> bytes` — encode (default mode), then utf-8/surrogateescape encode.

Keep it boring and readable; this ends up on a service tech's laptop.
